query: Insurance cost differences between kei cars and compact cars in Japan
brand_topic_category: Value, offers, finance and total cost of ownership

request_params_without_api_key.q: Insurance cost differences between kei cars and compact cars in Japan

search_parameters.q: Insurance cost differences between kei cars and compact cars in Japan

text_blocks.snippet: In Japan, car insurance is divided into Mandatory Insurance (Jibaiseki Hoken) and Voluntary Insurance (Nin-i Hoken). When comparing kei cars (yellow plate, ≤660cc) to compact cars (white plate, e.g., 1000cc–1500cc), kei cars are consistently cheaper to insure.
The cost differences and the structural reasons behind them are detailed below.
On average, a driver will save roughly ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 per year on voluntary insurance by choosing a kei car over a standard compact car.

table: Kei cars are ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 cheaper annually.
Premium gaps widen significantly for young profiles.

detailed.snippet: Kei cars are ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 cheaper annually.
Premium gaps widen significantly for young profiles.

formatted.cost_difference_details: Kei cars are ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 cheaper annually.
Premium gaps widen significantly for young profiles.

text_blocks.snippet: 2. Why Kei Car Voluntary Insurance is Cheaper

Voluntary premiums are determined using historical accident metrics calculated by the General Insurance Rating Organization of Japan (GIROJ). Kei cars benefit from lower rates due to three distinct variables:

list.snippet: Lower Accident Rates: Statistical data shows that kei cars historically hold a lower overall accident frequency compared to standard compact passenger vehicles.

Reduced Damage Scale: Because kei cars are physically lighter and limited in speed capability, they typically inflict less costly physical property and bodily damage to third parties during a collision.

Lower Vehicle Value (Sharyou Hoken): The replacement and repair costs for kei car parts are lower. If you opt for vehicle damage coverage, insuring a cheaper kei car reduces the risk payout ceiling for the provider.

text_blocks.snippet: Starting in recent years, Japan's insurance structure evaluates vehicle models through a tiered system known as Model-Specific Risk Class (Katashikibetsu Ryouritsu Kurasu). Insurance companies utilize this scale to dynamically adjust premiums:

list.snippet: Kei Cars: Scored on a simplified 1-to-7 scale across four coverage categories (Bodily injury, Property damage, Personal injury, and Vehicle damage).
Compact Cars: Scored on a more complex 1-to-17 scale.

The Impact: A highly popular compact car with a high collision history (e.g., certain sport-tuned compact variants) may sit at a much higher risk tier than a standard family-oriented kei car, widening the premium gap even further.

text_blocks.snippet: While a kei car is baseline cheaper, individual profile parameters heavily influence the final premium pricing:

list.snippet: Driver Age & Grade: Drivers under 21 or 26 face massive premiums regardless of vehicle type. Your cumulative insurance discount grade (Kykyu / 1–20 scale) heavily dictates final costs.

Provider Model: Choosing a Direct/Online insurer (such as SBI Sompo or Axa Direct) for a compact car is often significantly cheaper than using a traditional brick-and-mortar Agency insurer for a kei car.

text_blocks.snippet: To help get more precise expectations, are you planning to buy a brand new or used vehicle, and what is the age/license color of the primary driver?

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
In Japan, car insurance is divided into Mandatory Insurance \(Jibaiseki Hoken\) and Voluntary Insurance \(Nin\-i Hoken\). When comparing kei cars \(yellow plate, ≤660cc\) to compact cars \(white plate, e.g., 1000cc–1500cc\), kei cars are consistently cheaper to insure. [5] [1] [0] [3] [12]

The cost differences and the structural reasons behind them are detailed below. [13]

### 1. Cost Comparison Overview

On average, a driver will save roughly ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 per year on voluntary insurance by choosing a kei car over a standard compact car. [4]

| Insurance Type | Kei Cars \(≤660cc\) | Compact Cars \(1000cc–1500cc\) | Cost Difference Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Mandatory \(Jibaiseki\) | ¥17,540 \(For 24 months\) | ¥17,650 \(For 24 months\) | Negligible difference \(¥110 savings for kei\). |
| Voluntary \(Nin\-i\) | ¥20,000 – ¥50,000 / year \(Experienced driver\) | ¥40,000 – ¥80,000 / year \(Experienced driver\) | Kei cars are ¥6,000 to ¥23,000 cheaper annually. |
| Voluntary \(Young/New\) | ¥80,000 – ¥120,000 / year | ¥100,000 – ¥150,000\+ / year | Premium gaps widen significantly for young profiles. |
[1] [0] [4] [8] [2]

### 2. Why Kei Car Voluntary Insurance is Cheaper

Voluntary premiums are determined using historical accident metrics calculated by the General Insurance Rating Organization of Japan \(GIROJ\). Kei cars benefit from lower rates due to three distinct variables: [8] [14] [15] [16]

- Lower Accident Rates: Statistical data shows that kei cars historically hold a lower overall accident frequency compared to standard compact passenger vehicles.
- Reduced Damage Scale: Because kei cars are physically lighter and limited in speed capability, they typically inflict less costly physical property and bodily damage to third parties during a collision.
- Lower Vehicle Value \(Sharyou Hoken\): The replacement and repair costs for kei car parts are lower. If you opt for vehicle damage coverage, insuring a cheaper kei car reduces the risk payout ceiling for the provider.

### 3. Model\-Specific "Risk Class" Pricing Differences

Starting in recent years, Japan's insurance structure evaluates vehicle models through a tiered system known as Model\-Specific Risk Class \(Katashikibetsu Ryouritsu Kurasu\). Insurance companies utilize this scale to dynamically adjust premiums: [5] [8]

- Kei Cars: Scored on a simplified 1\-to\-7 scale across four coverage categories \(Bodily injury, Property damage, Personal injury, and Vehicle damage\).
- Compact Cars: Scored on a more complex 1\-to\-17 scale.
- The Impact: A highly popular compact car with a high collision history \(e.g., certain sport\-tuned compact variants\) may sit at a much higher risk tier than a standard family\-oriented kei car, widening the premium gap even further.

### 4. Other Factors Overriding Vehicle Type

While a kei car is baseline cheaper, individual profile parameters heavily influence the final premium pricing: [8] [0]

- Driver Age & Grade: Drivers under 21 or 26 face massive premiums regardless of vehicle type. Your cumulative insurance discount grade \(Kykyu / 1–20 scale\) heavily dictates final costs.
- Provider Model: Choosing a Direct/Online insurer \(such as SBI Sompo or Axa Direct\) for a compact car is often significantly cheaper than using a traditional brick\-and\-mortar Agency insurer for a kei car.

To help get more precise expectations, are you planning to buy a brand new or used vehicle, and what is the age/license color of the primary driver? [7] [11]
